display-lg.purpose: Page-level headings. Used once per page.

body-md.purpose: Standard text — table cells, descriptions, comments.

label-md.purpose: Table headers, metadata labels, button text.

code-sm.purpose: User IDs, report IDs, version numbers, timestamps.

shadows.name: modal-dropdown
shadows.purpose: Soft ambient shadow for modals, dropdowns, popovers.

motion.name: ease-hover
motion.purpose: Easing for hover and focus transitions (200ms).

components.name: Primary Button
components.description: Primary call to action. Used once per view.
components.css: .ds-btn-primary { background: #00288e; color: #ffffff; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 16px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 12px 24px; border: none; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-primary:hover { background: #1e40af; } .ds-btn-primary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #00288e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Secondary Button
components.css: .ds-btn-secondary { background: #ffffff; color: #1a1b22; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 16px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; padding: 12px 24px; border: 1px solid #c4c5d5; border-radius: 4px; cursor: pointer; transition: background 0.2s cubic-bezier(0.4,0,0.2,1); } .ds-btn-secondary:hover { background: #f4f2fc; } .ds-btn-secondary:focus-visible { outline: 2px solid #00288e; outline-offset: 2px; }

components.name: Text Input
components.description: Standard text input for forms and search.
components.css: .ds-label { font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; font-weight: 600; line-height: 16px; letter-spacing: 0.02em; color: #1a1b22; display: block; margin-bottom: 4px; } .ds-input { width: 100%; background: #ffffff; color: #1a1b22; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 14px; line-height: 20px; padding: 8px 12px; border: 1px solid #c4c5d5; border-radius: 4px; outline: none; transition: border-color 0.2s, box-shadow 0.2s; box-sizing: border-box; } .ds-input:focus { border-color: #00288e; box-shadow: 0 0 0 2px rgba(0,40,142,0.1); } .ds-input::placeholder { color: #c4c5d5; }

components.name: Status Badge — Pending
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-pending { background: #fef3c7; color: #92400e; }

components.name: Status Badge — Approved
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-approved { background: #d1fae5; color: #065f46; }

components.name: Status Badge — Rejected
components.css: .ds-badge { display: inline-flex; align-items: center; padding: 2px 8px; border-radius: 9999px; font-family: Inter, sans-serif; font-size: 12px; line-height: 16px; white-space: nowrap; } .ds-badge-rejected { background: #ffdad6; color: #93000a; }

components.name: Data Table Row
components.description: Standard table row with hover state.

components.html: Dashboard

narrative.overview: A precise and trustworthy tool. Every pixel in its right place. The interface feels like a well-organized workspace where nothing is surprising and everything is where you expect it — like a craftsman's bench arranged for efficiency, not decoration. ReportFlow is designed for high-density information environments where clarity, speed of comprehension, and reliability are paramount. The brand personality is authoritative yet secondary to the data it presents; the UI acts as a precise tool rather than a decorative layer.
keyCharacteristics: Systematic Minimalism — rigorous grid, deliberate whitespace, restrained color
Tonal layering over shadows — depth through color shifts, not drop shadows
Status as signal — color is reserved for workflow state, never decoration
Professional restraint — one accent color (Corporate Blue) used on ≤10% of surface area

rules.name: The One Voice Rule
rules.body: Primary blue is used on ≤10% of any given screen. Its rarity is the point — it directs attention to exactly one action per view.

rules.name: The Status-Only Rule
rules.body: Emerald, amber, and rose are reserved exclusively for workflow status signals. Never use them for decorative elements, link colors, or UI chrome.

rules.name: The Density Rule
rules.body: Body text never exceeds 14px in data views. The 12px label scale handles all metadata. This keeps data tables dense enough to be scannable without forcing horizontal scroll.

rules.name: The Flat-By-Default Rule
rules.body: Surfaces are flat at rest. Shadows appear only as a response to state (hover, focus) or for modals and dropdowns. A shadow on a resting card is a design error.

dos: Do use tonal layering for depth. Surface-container-low → surface-container → surface-container-high replaces shadows.
Do use the primary blue sparingly (≤10% of screen). Its rarity signals what matters.
Do keep tables dense. body-md (14px) for content, label-md (12px) for headers. Consistent 8px vertical padding.
Do pillar badges for status. Full-radius pill, 10% semantic background, full-color text.
Do show empty states with a brief message and the primary CTA when applicable.
Do use Inter with tabular numbers in data columns so figures align vertically.
donts: Don't use Material Design patterns — no floating action buttons, no heavy card shadows, no bottom navigation.
Don't apply border-left or border-right greater than 1px as a colored accent stripe on cards or list items. Use full borders, background tints, or nothing.
Don't use gradient text (background-clip: text). One solid color. Emphasis via weight or size.
Don't wrap everything in cards. Cards are for grouped data, not for every surface.
Don't use emerald, amber, or rose for anything other than workflow status. They are signal, not decoration.
Don't nest cards. A card inside a card is always wrong.
Don't use the hero-metric template (big number, small label, supporting stat) — it is a SaaS cliché.
Don't show audit trails in modals by default. The audit timeline should be visible inline or in a dedicated panel.
